title: Constrain indexFiles / documentIndex to safe filename charset — close nginx directive injection

verification.test: 336/336 workspace vitest (+20 new: 16 schema-base + 4 post-review for empty-string & dots-only edge cases)

teamFlow.reviewer: arceus:reviewer — 1 BLOCK (..dots-only) + 2 WARN + 3 SUGGEST; fixed before commit
teamFlow.checkSpec: Victini (check-spec) — re-run against the commit; see commit message for verdict

origin: check-spec multi-agent review (2026-05-27) — Layer 2 Websites/ACME/PMM review flagged that staticSitePayloadSchema and phpPayloadSchema allow unconstrained z.array(z.string()) for indexFiles / documentIndex, which the conf renderer interpolates directly into nginx config.
rootCause: packages/shared/src/schemas/websites.ts declares indexFiles and documentIndex as z.array(z.string()) with no per-item character constraint. apps/server/src/modules/websites/conf-renderer.ts writes them directly via 'index ${arr.join(" ")};' at lines 103 (static) and 126 (php). A site-create / site-update payload that contains an item like 'index.html; include /etc/passwd;' produces an nginx conf that includes a separate directive once it passes 'nginx -t'. The injected directive runs on the next nginx reload, with root context, against the host filesystem.
fixSummary: Tighten the schemas: indexFiles and documentIndex array items must match /^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$/ — the standard safe-filename charset, which is sufficient for every realistic index filename (index.html, index.htm, index.php, etc.). Also revise the conf-renderer's safety comment to reflect that the schema is now the actual enforcement layer (not just an aspiration).
